title: Google's extreme AI capex spending plans trigger a technical warning on the stock price
excerpt: Alphabet's stock price was the best-performing "Magnificent Seven" stock this year. Then earnings hit.
content_html:
Alphabet (GOOG, GOOGL) had been the best-performing "Magnificent Seven" stock of the year coming into its second quarter earnings release amid optimism that the tech giant was moving faster than most in monetizing AI initiatives. 

But that dynamic may be poised to change.

Quick insight: Alphabet shares closed below the key 100-day moving average for the first time since April on Wednesday, according to Yahoo Finance AlphaSpace data. The stock fell nearly 5% in premarket trading on Thursday as investors were spooked by Alphabet's capex spending trends. 

The downward move could put a test of the 200-day moving average at around $323 in play.

Alphabet's second quarter capital expenditures came in at $44.9 billion, slightly above Wall Street forecasts of $44.7 billion. Full-year capex guidance was raised to $195 billion to $205 billion from $180 billion to $190 billion, with a "significant" increase seen for 2027, executives said on the earnings call. 

Google's Q2, at a glance: Concerns on AI spending overshadowed a strong quarter for the company. The standout was Google Cloud, where revenue surged an eye-popping 82% to $24.8 billion, while the company's cloud backlog ballooned to $514 billion, underscoring the enormous demand for AI infrastructure.

CEO Sundar Pichai highlighted strong momentum across AI products, including AI-powered Search, YouTube, and Gemini, as the company continues to scale its AI ecosystem. 

"We continue to believe that Alphabet is the most well positioned of its peers to monetize its massive AI investment across all its properties by leveraging its proprietary TPU/Axion stack (and its major cost advantages over players such as Microsoft), its dominant distribution position, and its solid AI to take market share, cementing Gemini as the dominant AI platform on 4 billion plus handsets," Pivotal Research CEO and tech analyst Jeffrey Wlodarczak wrote in a note.

The bottom line: With capex spending plans by the hyperscalers getting intense scrutiny by investors, it's reasonable to expect Alphabet's stock to test the 200-day moving average.

Brian Sozzi is Yahoo Finance's Executive Editor, host of the Power Players with Brian Sozzi podcast, and a member of Yahoo Finance's editorial leadership team. Follow Sozzi on X @BrianSozzi, Instagram, and LinkedIn. Tips on stories? Email brian.sozzi@yahoofinance.com.

content_html_zh:
Alphabet（GOOG、GOOGL）在第二季度财报发布时成为今年表现最佳的“七巨头”股票，人们乐观地认为这家科技巨头在人工智能项目货币化方面的进展速度比大多数公司都快。

但这种动态可能即将发生变化。

快速洞察：根据雅虎财经 AlphaSpace 数据，Alphabet 股价周三自 4 月以来首次收于关键 100 日移动平均线下方。 由于投资者对 Alphabet 的资本支出趋势感到震惊，该股周四盘前交易中下跌近 5%。

此次下跌可能会考验 323 美元左右的 200 日移动均线。

Alphabet 第二季度资本支出为 449 亿美元，略高于华尔街预期的 447 亿美元。 高管们在财报电话会议上表示，全年资本支出指引从 1800 亿美元至 1900 亿美元上调至 1950 亿美元至 2050 亿美元，预计 2027 年将出现“显着”增长。

谷歌第二季度概览：对人工智能支出的担忧掩盖了该公司强劲的季度业绩。 表现最为突出的是谷歌云，其收入猛增 82%，达到 248 亿美元，而该公司的云积压订单激增至 5140 亿美元，凸显了对人工智能基础设施的巨大需求。

首席执行官 Sundar Pichai 强调了随着公司不断扩展其人工智能生态系统，人工智能产品的强劲势头，包括人工智能驱动的搜索、YouTube 和 Gemini。

Pivotal Research 首席执行官兼技术分析师 Jeffrey Wlodarczak 在一份报告中写道：“我们仍然相信，Alphabet 在同行中处于最有利的位置，可以利用其专有的 TPU/Axion 堆栈（以及相对于微软等公司的主要成本优势）、其主导的分销地位以及稳固的人工智能来夺取市场份额，从而巩固 Gemini 作为超过 40 亿部手机的主导人工智能平台的地位，从而将其在所有资产上的巨额人工智能投资货币化。”

底线：随着超大规模企业的资本支出计划受到投资者的严格审查，有理由预期 Alphabet 的股票将测试 200 天移动平均线。

Brian Sozzi 是雅虎财经的执行编辑、Brian Sozzi 播客的 Power Players 主持人，也是雅虎财经编辑领导团队的成员。 在 X @BrianSozzi、Instagram 和 LinkedIn 上关注 Sozzi。 关于故事的提示？ 电子邮件 brian.sozzi@yahoofinance.com。
